title: heracleum sphondylium f. stenophyllum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium f. stenophyllum, also known as wild celery or lesser meadow-sweet, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family, distinguished by its compact growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its health benefits include relieving digestive issues and reducing symptoms of menopausal hot flashes, in addition to its potential to lower blood sugar levels. Herbal preparations of Heracleum sphondylium f. stenophyllum can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile plant for various traditional medicine applications.
